LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SVETLANA STUKOV, AND, OLYMPIC REHABILITATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE ELDER NEGLECT SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S ACTIONS DO NOT RISE TO THE LEVEL OF ELDER NEGLECT DEFINED IN WELFARE & INSTITUTIONS CODE SECTION 15610.57, OR ACTIVITY PROTECTED AGAINST AS ENVISIONED IN THE ELDER ABUSE AND DEPENDENT ADULT CIVIL PROTECTION ACT. HEIGHTENED REMEDIES OF WELFARE & INSTITUTIONS CODE SECTION 15657 NOT AVAILABLE. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. NO CLEAR AND CONVINCING EVIDENCE OF RECKLESS, OPPRESSIVE, FRAUDULENT, OR MALICIOUS CONDUCT. (SEE DELANEY VS. BAKER (1999) 20 CAL.4TH 23, COVENANT CARE, INC. VS. SUPERIOR COURT (2004) 32 CAL.4TH 771 AND MACK VS. SOUNG (2000) 80 CAL.APP.4TH 966). (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SVETLANA STUKOV, and, OLYMPIC REHABILITATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Svetlana Shirinova, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 947-0703, and, Michael Kubicki, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 235-6675, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Jonathan A. Saul, Esq. of Thomas Minder & Assoc. (916) 929-4400, appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary adjudication of the Elder Neglect second cause of action, is granted. Defendant's actions do not rise to the level of elder neglect defined in Welfare & Institutions Code section 15610.57, or activity protected against as envisioned in the Elder Abuse and Dependent Adult Civil Protection Act. Heightened remedies of Welfare & Institutions Code section 15657 not available. Plaintiffs fail to raise a triable issue of material fact. No clear and convincing evidence of reckless, oppressive, fraudulent, or malicious conduct. (See Delaney vs. Baker (1999) 20 Cal.4th 23, Covenant Care, Inc. vs. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771 and Mack vs. Soung (2000) 80 Cal.App.4th 966).(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Svetlana M. Shirinova, Esq., and Martin Zurada, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Riva Ritsner
Jonathan A. Saul, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Olympic Rehabilitation

After hearing, Plaintiff Riva Ritsner's Motion for order compelling inspection of the premises and for sanctions in the amount of $1,250.00. is taken under submission. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $750.00
Off Calendar Jun-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Svetlana M. Shirinova, Esq., and Martin Zurada, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Riva Ritsner
Jonathan A. Saul, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Olympic Rehabilitation

After hearing, Plaintiff Riva Ritsner's Motion to compel depositions and request for sanctions in the amount of $750.00 is granted in part.
Deposition of Ms. Stukov to be completed by July 10, 2009; any further PMK deposition shall be completed within 7 days of the completion of the Stukov doposition.
Request for sanctions argued and sumitted. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
